mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity risk premiums and producing positive S&P 500 returns. The mechanism operates continuously whenever FEDFUNDS exhibits a downward trend over the preceding 3 months.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 1.91% suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations upward, producing positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking markets price in improved economic momentum.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer spending strength and housing investment. When both series exhibit positive momentum simultaneously, the combined demand impulse drives corporate profit expectations higher, producing positive 20-day forward returns. The mechanism requires both proxies to move in the same direction within a 2-month window.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show month-over-month growth > 0.5% within 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 507,533 million dollars, HOUST at 1,173 thousand units
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines signal supply-side weakness or demand destruction. When INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average by more than 1%, equity markets reprice downward as profit margin compression and reduced economic activity become evident, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average by > 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns during triggered periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, equity markets anticipate earnings headwinds and produce negative 20-day forward returns as forward guidance becomes more cautious.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation pressure (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary tightening regime. Under this regime, real discount rates remain elevated, suppressing equity valuations. The interaction of high inflation and restrictive policy produces negative 20-day forward returns as terminal rate expectations remain sticky.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year window, or negative 20-day returns occur in < 52% of joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly provides the broadest signal of economic expansion. Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDPC1 indicates sustained demand and profit growth, producing positive 60-day forward returns as the market prices in continued economic momentum and earnings expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 18,507.2 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns during positive GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal productive capacity utilization and labor deployment. When both series show synchronized growth above their 3-month moving averages, the economy operates at high capacity with tight labor markets, driving profit margins higher and producing positive 5-day forward returns as supply-side strength becomes evident.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both exceed their 3-month moving averages by > 0.5%
regime_hint: tight supply-demand balance with full capacity utilization
failure_condition: Rank IC of interaction term < 0.03 over any 2-year window, or positive 5-day returns occur in < 54% of joint-trigger instances.
